chapter: ENG9_4 – In the Kingdom of Fools
title: In the Kingdom of Fools – Easy SAQs

questions.question: Who ruled the Kingdom of Fools?
questions.answer: The Kingdom of Fools was ruled by a foolish king and his minister.

questions.question: What strange order did the king give to his people?
questions.answer: The king ordered the people to work at night and sleep during the day.

questions.question: Who arrived in the kingdom one day?
questions.answer: A guru and his disciple arrived in the kingdom.

questions.question: What surprised the guru and the disciple in the market?
questions.answer: They were surprised that everything in the market cost the same price, one duddu.

questions.question: Why did the guru want to leave the kingdom?
questions.answer: The guru wanted to leave because he realised it was a kingdom of fools.

questions.question: Why did the disciple stay in the kingdom?
questions.answer: The disciple stayed because food was very cheap.

questions.question: What happened to the thief in the merchant’s house?
questions.answer: The thief died when the wall of the house collapsed on him.

questions.question: Who complained to the king about the thief’s death?
questions.answer: The thief’s brother complained to the king.

questions.question: Why was the merchant called to the court?
questions.answer: The merchant was called because the thief died in his house.

questions.question: Who built the weak wall?
questions.answer: The wall was built by a bricklayer.

questions.question: Why did the bricklayer blame the dancing girl?
questions.answer: He said her jingling anklets distracted him while building the wall.

questions.question: Why did the dancing girl blame the goldsmith?
questions.answer: She blamed the goldsmith because he delayed making her jewellery.

questions.question: Why did the goldsmith blame the merchant?
questions.answer: The goldsmith said he was busy finishing the merchant’s order.

questions.question: Why could the merchant not be executed?
questions.answer: The merchant could not be executed because he was too thin for the stake.

questions.question: Who was chosen for execution instead?
questions.answer: The disciple was chosen for execution.

questions.question: Why was the disciple chosen for execution?
questions.answer: He was chosen because he was fat enough to fit the stake.

questions.question: How did the disciple feel when he was captured?
questions.answer: He felt frightened and remembered the guru’s warning.

questions.question: Who came to save the disciple?
questions.answer: The guru came to save him.

questions.question: What trick did the guru play on the king?
questions.answer: He said that whoever died first on the stake would become king in the next life.

questions.question: Why did the king want to die first?
questions.answer: He wanted to be reborn as king again.

questions.question: What happened to the king and the minister in the end?
questions.answer: They were executed on the stake.

questions.question: Who became the new king of the kingdom?
questions.answer: The guru became the new king.

questions.question: Who became the new minister?
questions.answer: The disciple became the new minister.

questions.question: What change did the guru make in the kingdom?
questions.answer: He restored normal day and night routines.

questions.question: What is the moral of the story?
questions.answer: Wisdom is more important than foolish decisions.
